title: Lifecycle Operation Stdout Envelope
description: Stdout envelope emitted by the cleanup, pin, unpin, and list orchestration scripts. Shares its core fields with specs/008-agent-runbook/contracts/orchestration-stdout.schema.json so agents can parse it with the same reader they use for run envelopes.

status.description: ok = operation succeeded (mutation performed or dry-run computed). refused = precondition failed (e.g., name collision, pin of a run with no manifest, concurrent run in progress). failed = an unexpected error occurred; diagnostics[] explains.

failureKind.description: Populated when status is 'refused' or 'failed'; null on success.

operation.description: Which lifecycle operation emitted this envelope.

dryRun.description: True when the caller passed -DryRun / -WhatIf. When true, plannedPaths[] describes what would happen; no filesystem mutation has occurred.

plannedPaths.description: Audit trail of every filesystem path the operation touched (or would touch in dry-run mode). Project-root-relative.

action.description: What the operation did to (or would do to) this path. 'skip' means the path was considered but not touched (e.g., already absent during cleanup).

pinName.description: Populated for pin and unpin responses; null for cleanup; null for list (the index itself names the pins).

pinnedRunIndex.description: Populated only by the list operation; null for cleanup/pin/unpin. When present, shape matches pinned-run-index.schema.json #/$defs/PinRecord items.

diagnostics.description: Zero-or-more human-readable diagnostic strings. Used to surface stale-marker recovery, partial-cleanup warnings, and refusal reasons.

completedAt.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp when the operation emitted its envelope.

manifestPath.description: Present-and-null for lifecycle operations so shared envelope readers do not need a special code path. Real run envelopes populate this field; lifecycle envelopes always leave it null.
